title: Init helper rebuilds ignored JSON, not runtime SQLite
evidence: .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/init-skill-graph.sh:55-62 validates metadata, exports skill-graph.json, then runs health; .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py:929-950 loads only SQLite and logs "JSON export ignored for runtime"; .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/tests/migration-lineage-identity.vitest.ts:172-176 asserts missing SQLite plus JSON yields error "sqlite_missing_json_ignored".
detail: The operational-looking `init-skill-graph.sh` does not initialize or rebuild the runtime `skill-graph.sqlite`; it refreshes the legacy JSON artifact that the runtime intentionally ignores. An operator following this script after a missing or corrupt DB can finish with a fresh JSON file while advisor routing still has no usable skill graph.
fix_sketch: Change the init/recovery helper to invoke the trusted SQLite rebuild path (`advisor_rebuild` or `skill_graph_scan`) and either remove JSON export from the recovery flow or label it validation-only.

title: Read-only skill graph status can mutate corrupt DB state
evidence: .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/handlers/skill-graph/status.ts:56-59 calls skillGraphDb.getDb() for status; .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/lib/skill-graph/skill-graph-db.ts:359-363 runs integrity recovery during initDb; .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/lib/skill-graph/skill-graph-db.ts:289-300 renames the DB to *.corrupt or deletes it if rename fails.
detail: `skill_graph_status` is a diagnostic/read surface, but it opens the read-write DB initializer. On a corrupt file, merely asking for status can move or delete the live SQLite artifact before an operator snapshots it, which violates the expected runbook boundary between diagnosis and recovery.
fix_sketch: Make status/query validation use a read-only integrity probe first and require an explicit trusted repair command before renaming, deleting, or recreating `skill-graph.sqlite`.

title: advisor_rebuild can skip corrupt SQLite when generation says live
evidence: .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/handlers/advisor-status.ts:207-210 only checks DB path existence for status; .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/handlers/advisor-status.ts:244-252 derives freshness from generation/source-change state, not SQLite quick_check; .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/handlers/advisor-rebuild.ts:48-52 skips rebuild when freshness is live and trustState is not absent; corruption recovery only happens later inside initDb at skill-graph-db.ts:359-363 if indexing is actually reached.
detail: A corrupt `skill-graph.sqlite` with a still-live generation record can be reported as live because `advisor_status` does not integrity-check the DB. A non-force `advisor_rebuild` then skips, so the documented explicit repair path is bypassed unless the operator already knows to force rebuild.
fix_sketch: Include a bounded SQLite integrity result in `advisor_status`/trustState and make `advisor_rebuild` rebuild whenever the DB is corrupt, regardless of generation freshness.

title: Rebuild ownership docs contradict watcher behavior
evidence: .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/README.md:106-108 says the daemon invalidates cache and "does not rebuild on its own" and "Only advisor_rebuild mutates the SQLite database"; .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/ARCHITECTURE.md:113 and :129 say the daemon triggers incremental rebuilds; actual code wires watcher reindexing through indexSkillMetadata at .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/lib/daemon/watcher.ts:425-528 and publishes live generation at watcher-orchestrator.ts:100-124.
detail: The docs disagree on whether the daemon is only a freshness marker or an automatic SQLite writer. The code shows the watcher can reindex and publish a live generation, which matters for operational guidance around single-writer discipline, corruption recovery, and when a manual rebuild is required.
fix_sketch: Update the runbook and architecture docs to state the exact writer set: trusted tools plus daemon watcher reindexing, with guidance for pausing/recovering around those writers.
